title: Internal error in V3Gate dedup with -fno-inline on tri0/tri1 ports
body:
## Bug

With `-fno-inline`, Verilator hits an internal assertion in V3Gate's dedup visitor when compiling modules that use `tri0`/`tri1` declarations on input ports with `buf` gate primitives (common in Altera/Intel LPM vendor IP).

```
%Error: Internal Error: V3Gate.cpp:961: Consumer doesn't match lhs of assign
```

### Failing tests (6)

All use modules from `t/t_altera_lpm.v`:

- `t_altera_lpm_bustri`
- `t_altera_lpm_counter`
- `t_altera_lpm_decode`
- `t_altera_lpm_ff`
- `t_altera_lpm_ram_io`
- `t_altera_lpm_rom`

### Reproducer

```bash
cd test_regress
perl ../bin/verilator --cc --top-module lpm_bustri -fno-inline t/t_altera_lpm.v
```

### Related

- #3258 / PR #3268 — same assertion, different trigger (inout ports with `no_inline_module`)
- Found during the `-fno-inline` audit for #1539 (post-scope inliner)

### Version

Tested on master at `bb0e1c8c6`.
code_blocks:
%Error: Internal Error: V3Gate.cpp:961: Consumer doesn't match lhs of assign
cd test_regress
perl ../bin/verilator --cc --top-module lpm_bustri -fno-inline t/t_altera_lpm.v
